---
title: "Peripheral Blood Cell Classification with Foundation Model Features"
author: "Chung Yan Yu (Joel)"
date: today
format:
  pdf:
    toc: false
    number-sections: true
    colorlinks: true
  docx:
    toc: false
    number-sections: true
bibliography: references.bib
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
---

```{python}
#| label: setup
#| include: false

import sys
from pathlib import Path

# Add scripts/ to path so we can import from config, etc.
sys.path.insert(0, str(Path("../scripts").resolve()))

import json
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

from config import (
    BACKBONES, BACKBONE_DISPLAY, CLASS_ORDER, CLASS_ORDER_ALPHA,
    CLASS_LABELS, COLOURS, SPLIT_ORDER, style_axis, load_features,
)

RESULTS_DIR = Path("../results")
```

## Abstract


## Introduction



## Methods

### Dataset

```{python}
#| label: fig-class-distribution
#| fig-cap: "Class distribution across train/validation/test splits in the KU-Optofil dataset."

from PIL import Image
img = Image.open(RESULTS_DIR / "class_by_split.png")
plt.figure(figsize=(10, 5))
plt.imshow(img)
plt.axis("off")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

### Feature Extraction


### Frozen-Feature Classifiers

#### XGBoost

```{python}
#| label: tbl-xgboost-params
#| tbl-cap: "Best hyperparameters found by Optuna (TPE sampler) for each backbone."

xgb_results = json.load(open(RESULTS_DIR / "xgboost_results.json"))

rows = []
for bb in BACKBONES:
    if bb not in xgb_results:
        continue
    params = xgb_results[bb]["best_params"]
    rows.append({
        "Backbone": BACKBONE_DISPLAY.get(bb, bb),
        "n_estimators": params["n_estimators"],
        "max_depth": params["max_depth"],
        "learning_rate": f"{params['learning_rate']:.4f}",
        "subsample": f"{params['subsample']:.2f}",
        "colsample_bytree": f"{params['colsample_bytree']:.2f}",
        "Trials": xgb_results[bb]["tpe_trials"],
        "Val macro F1": f"{xgb_results[bb]['best_val_macro_f1']:.4f}",
    })

pd.DataFrame(rows)
```

#### Linear Probe


### End-to-End Fine-Tuning


## Results

### Headline Comparison

```{python}
#| label: tbl-headline
#| tbl-cap: "Test set performance across all models (mean +/- std over 5 seeds)."

lp_results = json.load(open(RESULTS_DIR / "linear_probe_results.json"))
xgb_results = json.load(open(RESULTS_DIR / "xgboost_results.json"))

# Load fine-tuning results if available
ft_path = RESULTS_DIR / "finetune_results.json"
ft_results = json.load(open(ft_path)) if ft_path.exists() else {}

rows = []
for bb in BACKBONES:
    for method, data in [("Linear Probe", lp_results), ("XGBoost", xgb_results), ("Fine-tuned", ft_results)]:
        if bb not in data:
            continue
        tr = data[bb]["test_results"]
        rows.append({
            "Backbone": BACKBONE_DISPLAY.get(bb, bb),
            "Classifier": method,
            "Macro F1": f"{tr['macro_f1_mean']:.4f} +/- {tr['macro_f1_std']:.4f}",
            "Weighted F1": f"{tr['weighted_f1_mean']:.4f} +/- {tr['weighted_f1_std']:.4f}",
            "Accuracy": f"{tr['accuracy_mean']:.4f} +/- {tr['accuracy_std']:.4f}",
            "Balanced Acc": f"{tr['balanced_accuracy_mean']:.4f} +/- {tr['balanced_accuracy_std']:.4f}",
        })

pd.DataFrame(rows)
```

### Per-Class Performance

```{python}
#| label: fig-perclass-f1
#| fig-cap: "Per-class F1 scores for each backbone-classifier combination."

# Build per-class F1 dataframe from classification reports
rows = []
for bb in BACKBONES:
    for method, data in [("Linear Probe", lp_results), ("XGBoost", xgb_results), ("Fine-tuned", ft_results)]:
        if bb not in data:
            continue
        report = data[bb]["test_results"]["classification_report"]
        for cls in CLASS_ORDER_ALPHA:
            if cls in report:
                rows.append({
                    "Class": CLASS_LABELS.get(cls, cls),
                    "Backbone": BACKBONE_DISPLAY.get(bb, bb),
                    "Classifier": method,
                    "F1": report[cls]["f1-score"],
                })

df = pd.DataFrame(rows)

if not df.empty:
    pivot = df.pivot_table(index="Class", columns=["Backbone", "Classifier"], values="F1")
    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(12, 6))
    pivot.plot(kind="barh", ax=ax)
    style_axis(ax)
    ax.set_xlabel("F1 Score")
    ax.set_title("Per-Class F1 by Backbone and Classifier")
    ax.legend(bbox_to_anchor=(1.05, 1), loc="upper left", fontsize=8)
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.show()
```

### Confusion Matrices

```{python}
#| label: fig-confusion-matrices
#| fig-cap: "Confusion matrices for the best-performing configuration per backbone (XGBoost, median seed)."

from matplotlib.colors import LogNorm

fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 3, figsize=(18, 5))

for ax, bb in zip(axes, BACKBONES):
    if bb not in xgb_results:
        continue
    cm = np.array(xgb_results[bb]["test_results"]["confusion_matrix"])
    # Normalise by row (true class)
    cm_norm = cm.astype(float) / cm.sum(axis=1, keepdims=True)

    im = ax.imshow(cm_norm, cmap="Blues", vmin=0, vmax=1)
    ax.set_title(BACKBONE_DISPLAY.get(bb, bb))
    ax.set_xlabel("Predicted")
    ax.set_ylabel("True")

    labels = [CLASS_LABELS.get(c, c) for c in CLASS_ORDER_ALPHA]
    ax.set_xticks(range(len(labels)))
    ax.set_xticklabels(labels, rotation=90, fontsize=7)
    ax.set_yticks(range(len(labels)))
    ax.set_yticklabels(labels, fontsize=7)

fig.colorbar(im, ax=axes, shrink=0.6, label="Recall (row-normalised)")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

### Optuna Convergence

```{python}
#| label: fig-optuna
#| fig-cap: "Optuna TPE convergence plots for XGBoost HP tuning."

fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 3, figsize=(15, 4))

for ax, bb in zip(axes, BACKBONES):
    img_path = RESULTS_DIR / f"optuna_history_{bb}.png"
    if img_path.exists():
        img = Image.open(img_path)
        ax.imshow(img)
    ax.set_title(BACKBONE_DISPLAY.get(bb, bb))
    ax.axis("off")

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

### Training Curves (Fine-Tuning)

```{python}
#| label: fig-training-curves
#| fig-cap: "Training loss and validation macro F1 over epochs for the best Optuna trial per backbone."

# TODO: extract training history from fine-tuning results once available
# ft_results[backbone]["training_history"] would contain per-epoch data
```

## Discussion


## References

::: {#refs}
:::
